title: Buy-a-Tesla aspiration list
html:
TL;DR

  • Late-October-2021 freshman OSU aspiration note: buy a Tesla, have consistent NBA front-row seats, order takeout without thinking, take family and friends on vacation — closes with "what is your top priority this week?"
  • All four wishes resolve to the same underlying thing: money that buys frictionlessness — Tesla = transport without compromise, front-row = access, takeout-without-care = no menu-pricing math, vacation-for-family = the mom house thread expressed as travel.
  • Continuous with the three things manifest two days later and the Buffett vs Bezos note six days earlier — late-October 2021 is Yahir's what-do-I-actually-want sprint.
  • The closing self-prompt — "what is your top priority this week?" — is the right question discipline applied to himself; the aspiration list is the answer-attempt.
  • First Tesla mention in vault as a personal want (vs. the Tesla vs Edison founder-archetype framing the same week) — the car-as-status-symbol vs. the inventor-as-archetype, both alive in Yahir's head simultaneously.

Note

Four sentences of pure want. Yahir at 18 listing the markers he'd use to know he'd "made it" — the markers framing applied to material life. Notable: family/friends vacation appears alongside the Tesla, not after it; the gratitude payback instinct is already structuring even the surface-level wishlist. The terminal self-question is the move that distinguishes this from a daydream — Yahir is reverse-engineering this-week priorities from long-arc desires.

Connections

  • no runway discipline — wishes-as-priorities
  • mom house by the river goal — vacation-for-family thread
  • 2021 10 19 setting your heart ablaze — Tesla-as-archetype companion piece
  • 2021 10 26 three things manifest — same-week life-frame note

related.label: Setting your heart ablaze — Tesla vs Edison
